items.html: This is what is written in The Zohar, parashat Balak, p 203: "'I behold him [Ashurenu], but not near' — since he was in his mother's innards, Ashurenu; he extended a step outward [Pesia LeVar]," etc. — derived from "if my step [Ashuri] has turned out of Your way," the language of a step. The matter is that at the time of the first Ibur [conception], ZON [Zeir Anpin and Nukva] were in the discernment of the small Vav within the first Hey of the Name. That Vav inside the Hey has no head, for ZA was still only in a discernment of VAK [six ends] without a head. Moreover, they are not a large Vav, in which each of the six ends would be revealed; rather, the three were incorporated in the three, as above, for this is the very purpose of the correction during the time of the Ibur. Therefore it is a small Vav, half the measure of the letter Vav, and it is called the small Vav. And then, at the end of this Vav, in its middle line, Malchut was clothing the end of that line, and there she was in the discernment of that lower tip that protrudes slightly outward from the end of this letter Vav. This is what is called Pesia LeVar [a step outward], since it is at the end of the leg of this Vav, protruding slightly outward — so that the thickness of three points would be there, unlike the two other lines.
